question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: flywayConfigurationCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted `hiero.mirror.importer.network` value (e.g., 'MAINNET' with mixed case) to the Spring environment that causes `HederaNetwork.MAINNET.equalsIgnoreCase(importerProperties.getNetwork())` to return true on a non-mainnet node, injecting the mainnet `topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp` (1592499600000000000L) into the Flyway placeholder, causing the V1.23.1 migration to assign `running_hash_version=1` to topic messages that should be version 2, permanently corrupting the Hashgraph topic running-hash chain?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: flywayConfigurationCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a crafted `hiero.mirror.importer.topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp` property value of `0` via an externally-readable configuration source, causing the Flyway placeholder `topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp` to be set to `0`, so that the V1.23.1 migration assigns `running_hash_version=2` to ALL historical topic messages regardless of their actual consensus timestamp, making every pre-v2 topic message hash unverifiable and corrupting the entire topic running-hash history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: flywayConfigurationCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user set `hiero.mirror.importer.topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp` to `9999999999999999999` (Long.MAX_VALUE-like value) via an externally-exposed configuration endpoint, causing the Flyway migration V1.23.1 to assign `running_hash_version=1` to ALL topic messages including those that should be version 2, silently invalidating all post-upgrade topic message running hashes stored in the mirror node database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: flywayConfigurationCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `importerProperties.getNetwork()` strips a prefix delimiter (e.g., 'mainnet-custom' becomes 'mainnet') to craft a network string like 'mainnet-attacker' that passes the `equalsIgnoreCase` check, causing the mainnet-specific `topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp` to be injected into a non-mainnet deployment's Flyway migration, corrupting the running_hash_version boundary for all topic messages?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: flywayConfigurationCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user who can influence the Spring Boot external configuration (e.g., via environment variable `HIERO_MIRROR_IMPORTER_TOPICRUNNINGHASHV2ADDEDTIMESTAMP`) set a negative timestamp value, causing the Flyway SQL placeholder substitution in V1.23.1 to produce an invalid `CASE WHEN consensus_timestamp < <negative> THEN 1 ELSE 2 END` expression that assigns `running_hash_version=2` to all records, corrupting the historical hash version assignment for all topic messages?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: flywayConfigurationCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of validation on `topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp` in `ImporterProperties` (the field has no `@Min`/`@Max` constraint) to supply a value that is exactly 1 nanosecond off from the true network upgrade timestamp, causing the migration to misclassify exactly the boundary topic messages, introducing an undetectable single-record running-hash version corruption in the Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: flywayConfigurationCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a re-run of Flyway repeatable migrations (by manipulating the `hiero.mirror.importer.migration.<name>.checksum` property) while simultaneously having set a malicious `topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp`, causing the V1.23.1 migration to re-execute with the wrong timestamp and overwrite previously correct `running_hash_version` values in the `topic_message` table, permanently corrupting the Hashgraph topic history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: flywayConfigurationCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the mutable `configuration.getPlaceholders()` map returned by Flyway's configuration object to inject additional SQL-injection-style content into the `topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp` placeholder value (e.g., `0 OR 1=1`), causing the V1.23.1 migration SQL `CASE WHEN consensus_timestamp < ${topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp}` to execute unintended SQL logic that corrupts running_hash_version assignments across all topic messages?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: flywayConfigurationCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user who can set `hiero.mirror.importer.network` to a value that is neither 'mainnet' nor any recognized network (e.g., 'other') cause the `flywayConfigurationCustomizer()` to always use the non-mainnet default timestamp `1588706343553042000L`, and if this is deployed against a mainnet mirror node, cause all topic messages between the two timestamps to receive the wrong `running_hash_version`, corrupting the running hash chain for that time window?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `setInitializationFailTimeout(-1)` setting on `flywayHikariConfig` to cause the Flyway DataSource to silently connect to a rogue database (by influencing the JDBC URL via `JdbcConnectionDetails`) without failing at startup, allowing Flyway migrations to run against a tampered database schema that corrupts the Hashgraph history tables?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user who can influence the `JdbcConnectionDetails` bean (e.g., via a cloud metadata service SSRF or environment variable injection) supply a malicious JDBC URL to `connectionDetails.getJdbcUrl()`, redirecting the Flyway owner-level DataSource to a rogue PostgreSQL server that returns tampered migration results, causing the importer to believe schema migrations succeeded while the actual Hashgraph history tables are corrupted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `hikariConfig.copyStateTo(flywayHikariConfig)` call to inherit a pre-configured `connectionInitSql` from `DBProperties` that executes arbitrary SQL on the owner-level Flyway connection at pool initialization, allowing manipulation of the `flyway_schema_history` table to mark history-corrupting migrations as already applied, bypassing Flyway's integrity checks?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `dbWaiter.waitForDatabase(jdbcUrl, dbProperties.getOwner(), dbProperties.getOwnerPassword())` call—which uses `DriverManager.getConnection()` with the owner password—to perform a timing-based credential oracle attack by observing the retry interval behavior, eventually recovering the owner-level database password and gaining direct write access to Hashgraph history tables?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a pool exhaustion denial-of-service by triggering repeated application restarts (e.g., via a public health endpoint) that each create a new `HikariDataSource` with `setMaximumPoolSize(10)` Flyway connections, exhausting the PostgreSQL connection limit and preventing legitimate Flyway migrations from running, leaving the Hashgraph history schema in a partially-migrated corrupt state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `flywayHikariConfig.setIdleTimeout(60000)` keeps Flyway owner-level connections alive for 60 seconds after use, and if the application is running in a shared environment, use a side-channel to hijack an idle owner-level connection and execute DDL statements that alter the `topic_message` table's `running_hash_version` column, corrupting the Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `dataSourceProperties.determineUrl()` fallback (used when `connectionDetails` is null) to inject a JDBC URL containing additional PostgreSQL connection parameters (e.g., `?options=-c%20search_path%3Dattacker_schema`) that redirect Flyway migrations to a different schema, causing Hashgraph history tables to be created in an attacker-controlled schema while the legitimate schema remains uninitialized?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@PostConstruct init()` check—which only validates `blockProperties.isEnabled() && recordDownloaderProperties.isEnabled()` at startup—by triggering a runtime property refresh (via Spring Cloud Config or Actuator) that enables both block and record downloaders simultaneously after startup, causing dual-ingestion of Hashgraph stream files that produces duplicate or conflicting history records in the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `init()` throws `IllegalStateException` when both block and record downloaders are enabled to cause a repeated application crash-loop (by ensuring both are enabled via environment variables), preventing the importer from ever processing legitimate Hashgraph stream files and creating a persistent denial-of-service against history ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user who can set `hiero.mirror.importer.block.enabled=false` and `hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.record.enabled=false` simultaneously bypass the `init()` guard entirely, causing the importer to start with no stream source active, silently halting all Hashgraph history ingestion without any error, leading to a stale/incomplete mirror node history that consumers treat as authoritative?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: flywayConfigurationCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `configuration.getPlaceholders()` returns a mutable map and that `flywayConfigurationCustomizer()` is a Spring bean (potentially called multiple times in certain Spring contexts) to register a second `FlywayConfigurationCustomizer` bean that overwrites the `topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp` placeholder with a malicious value after `ImporterConfiguration`'s customizer runs, corrupting the migration timestamp used for hash version assignment?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: flywayConfigurationCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Spring Boot `@ConfigurationProperties` binding for `hiero.mirror.importer.topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp` via a YAML/properties file placed in a Spring-scanned location (e.g., `./config/application.yml`) to override the timestamp to a value that causes the Flyway V1.23.1 migration to assign incorrect `running_hash_version` values, corrupting the topic message running hash chain used to verify Hashgraph consensus history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: flywayConfigurationCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `Long.toString()` conversion of the timestamp in `configuration.getPlaceholders().put('topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp', timestamp.toString())` to inject a value that, when substituted into the Flyway SQL `CASE WHEN consensus_timestamp < ${topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp}`, causes a PostgreSQL type coercion error that silently skips the `running_hash_version` update, leaving all topic messages with NULL hash versions and breaking hash chain verification?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `DBProperties.sslMode` defaulting to `SslMode.DISABLE` to perform a man-in-the-middle attack on the Flyway owner-level database connection, intercepting and modifying Flyway migration SQL in transit to alter the `running_hash_version` assignment logic in V1.23.1, causing the migration to corrupt the Hashgraph topic message hash history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `DBProperties.loadBalance=true` default to redirect the Flyway owner-level connection to a secondary/replica database node that has stale or tampered `flyway_schema_history` records, causing Flyway to skip critical migrations (including those that set `running_hash_version`) and leaving the Hashgraph history tables in a corrupted state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: flywayConfigurationCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@AutoConfigureBefore(FlywayAutoConfiguration.class)` ordering to register a competing `FlywayConfigurationCustomizer` bean with higher precedence (via `@Order` or `@Primary`) that runs before `ImporterConfiguration`'s customizer and sets `topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp` to a malicious value, which then gets overwritten—but if the attacker's customizer runs after, it permanently corrupts the hash version boundary used in Flyway migrations?
